meta.name: IUS-12 Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale Short Form
meta.description: The IUS-12 is a 12-item short form of the Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale that measures reactions to ambiguous situations, uncertainty, and future events. It assesses two factors: Prospective Anxiety (desire for predictability) and Inhibitory Anxiety (paralysis of action due to uncertainty).
meta.author: Carleton, Norton & Asmundson (2007)
meta.reference: Carleton RN, Norton MA, Asmundson GJ. Fearing the unknown: A short version of the Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale. J Anxiety Disord. 2007;21(1):105-117.
meta.instruction: Please select the answer that best corresponds to how much each statement is characteristic of you.

commonChoices.label: Not at all characteristic of me

questions.text: It frustrates me not having all the information I need.
Uncertainty keeps me from living a full life.
One should always look ahead so as to avoid surprises.
A small unforeseen event can spoil everything, even with the best of planning.
When it's time to act, uncertainty paralyzes me.
When I am uncertain, I can't function very well.
I always want to know what the future has in store for me.
I can't stand being taken by surprise.
The smallest doubt can stop me from acting.
I should be able to organize everything in advance.
I must get away from all uncertain situations.

ranges.label: Low Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is low. You appear to cope well with ambiguous and unpredictable situations. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue leveraging your flexibility in uncertain situations
Maintain healthy coping strategies
Use your adaptability as a strength in challenging circumstances
Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching

ranges.label: Moderate Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of intolerance of uncertainty. Some ambiguous situations may cause you discomfort or worry. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Practice accepting uncertainty as a normal part of life
Challenge catastrophic thinking about unknown outcomes
Try gradual exposure to uncertain situations
Develop flexible planning strategies
Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist

ranges.label: High Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is high. Ambiguity and unpredictability likely cause you significant distress and may interfere with daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider cognitive-behavioral strategies to address uncertainty-related anxiety
Practice mindfulness to stay grounded in the present moment
Work on tolerating small uncertainties and gradually increasing exposure
Consult a mental health professional for structured support
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible

ranges.label: Very High Intolerance of Uncertainty
ranges.description: Your intolerance of uncertainty is very high. This level of discomfort with uncertainty may significantly impair your daily life and well-being. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly consider seeking professional help from a therapist experienced in anxiety disorders
Explore evidence-based treatments such as CBT or metacognitive therapy
Implement daily anxiety management routines
Avoid excessive reassurance-seeking, which can maintain the cycle
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible

prospective.label: Low
prospective.description: Low prospective anxiety. You handle anticipation of future events well. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

prospective.label: Moderate
prospective.description: Moderate prospective anxiety. You sometimes worry about future uncertainties. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

prospective.label: High
prospective.description: High prospective anxiety. The unpredictability of the future causes you significant concern. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

prospective.label: Very High
prospective.description: Very high prospective anxiety. You experience intense distress about future uncertainties. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

inhibitory.label: Low
inhibitory.description: Low inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty does not significantly hinder your actions. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

inhibitory.label: Moderate
inhibitory.description: Moderate inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty sometimes holds you back from taking action. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

inhibitory.label: High
inhibitory.description: High inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty frequently paralyzes your decision-making and actions. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

inhibitory.label: Very High
inhibitory.description: Very high inhibitory anxiety. Uncertainty severely impairs your ability to act and make decisions. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system
